title: phegopteris manipurensis
intro: Phegopteris manipurensis is a species of fern, commonly known as the Manipuri chain fern, which is native to the northeastern parts of India and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Phegopteris manipurensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory disorders, such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in poultices and topical applications for skin issues and wounds.
